Show that the following arguments are invalid by providing diagrammatic
counterexamples. In your diagrams, use arrows to represent the relation $R$,
circle any points that have the property $P$, and draw a square around any
points that have the property $Q$.

a. $\exists x(\forall y (Rxy))\proves \forall x(\exists y(Rxy))$

b. $\forall x(\exists y (Rxy))\proves \exists x(\forall y(Ryx))$

c. $\forall x(Px\imp Qx)\proves \forall x(Px)\imp \forall x(Qx)$

d. $\forall x(\exists y (Py\wedge Rxy))\proves\exists y(\forall x(Py\wedge Rxy))$

e. $\forall x(P(x)\imp \exists y(Rxy))\proves\forall x(\exists y(Py\wedge Rxy))$
